evidence: The paper calculates variable importance via leave-one-out cross-validation on the full dataset (S4 Fig caption), not via tree impurity (Gini/MDI). It reports that certain variables are 'most important' (e.g., 'year being the most important predictor variable when included' in Scenario B), but this validation-based importance is robust to impurity bias. The importance findings support model interpretation but are not central to the core conclusion about dengue serotype prediction accuracy (76-81%), which is validated through held-out test sets (100 bootstrap samples, 90/10 train/test split).
